---
title: "Prompt-injection classifier: what the evaluation found"
author: "Brandon Behring"
date: "2026-05-19"
description: "Plain-language landing page for the prompt-injection classifier evaluation."
---

# Prompt-injection classifier: what the evaluation found

Prompt injection is untrusted text that tries to override the instructions an
LLM system is supposed to follow. This project asks a narrow question: if we
train several simple prompt-injection detectors on direct instruction-override
examples, do they still work when the attack family changes?

The short answer is **mostly no**. The evaluation is useful because it makes
that failure visible instead of hiding it behind an in-domain score.

Here, **out-of-distribution (OOD)** means examples drawn from attack families
that were absent or underrepresented in training.

## What Was Evaluated

- **Task**: single-turn English text classification: injection vs benign.
- **Training style**: four direct-injection sources, such as "ignore the
  previous instructions" attacks.
- **OOD test style**: five held-out slices covering indirect injection,
  multi-turn agentic flow, jailbreak-style questions, and benign text that
  only looks injection-shaped.
- **Models**: a TF-IDF + logistic-regression floor, a frozen ModernBERT probe,
  a ModernBERT LoRA fine-tune, and two ProtectAI reference detectors.
- **Non-goal**: this is not a deployment recommendation and not a leaderboard.

## Headline Result

The main metric is **AUPRC**, which is useful for imbalanced binary
classification. On this pooled OOD slice, a random ranking scores **0.374**
because 412 of 1101 examples are positive. A detector needs to beat that floor
to show useful ranking signal.

| Detector | Pooled OOD AUPRC | 95% CI | Read |
|---|---:|---:|---|
| ModernBERT frozen probe | **0.364** | [0.354, 0.375] | Best in-house score, still at the random floor |
| ProtectAI v1 | 0.361 | [0.330, 0.391] | Similar, but reference scorer has training-overlap caveats |
| ProtectAI v2 | 0.314 | [0.283, 0.345] | Worse than v1 on this slate |
| ModernBERT LoRA | 0.293 | [0.286, 0.301] | Fine-tuning hurt OOD ranking |
| TF-IDF + LR | 0.291 | [0.283, 0.298] | Classical floor, roughly tied with LoRA |

> *Reading the table*: **Pooled OOD AUPRC** is the area under the precision-recall curve on the combined out-of-distribution (OOD) test slices — five held-out attack and benign sources (BIPIA, InjecAgent, JBB-Behaviors, XSTest, NotInject) that no detector saw during training. A random ranking on this slate scores ≈ **0.374** (the positive-class prevalence, 412/1101). Any detector at or below the random floor is not clearly better than guessing. The 95% confidence interval (CI) width is the noise floor for a comparison; if two detectors' CIs overlap heavily, the difference between them is not statistically meaningful.

## What This Means

1. **The failure is cross-family.** The models saw direct prompt injections in
   training, but the OOD slate includes attack families they did not see.
2. **Fine-tuning did not help.** LoRA fits the direct-injection training pool
   and loses much of the OOD signal present in the frozen backbone.
3. **A newer detector is not automatically better.** ProtectAI v2 improves one
   slice but regresses on another.
4. **Thresholds do not transfer cleanly.** A threshold tuned for 1% false
   positives on validation can produce much higher false-positive rates on
   held-out sources.
5. **The honest result is negative.** The project demonstrates the evaluation
   harness and the limits of the trained detectors, not a ready-to-ship model.

## Read Next

- **For hiring managers in a hurry → [60-second tour](docs/for-hiring-managers.md)**:
  4 questions (what problem / what found / why trust / how the candidate
  thinks). Best entry point if you have a minute and want the headline +
  the methodology signal.
- [Results](RESULTS.md): exact tables, five canonical figures, and raw artifact
  pointers.
- [Executive summary](EXECUTIVE_SUMMARY.md): one-page decision-maker version.
- [Writeup](WRITEUP.md): methodology narrative and links to detailed spokes.
- [Reading guide](READING_GUIDE.md): paths for quick review, audit, and
  reproduction.
